title: jasminum dispermum subsp. forrestianum
intro: Jasminum dispermum subsp. forrestianum, also known as the Himalayan jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
